latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9939T
Victoria PLC
23 February 2026
23 February 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
Victoria PLC
('Victoria' or the 'Company')
Trading Update
Victoria PLC (LSE: VCP), the international designer, manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring, provides a trading update and outlook for FY2026.
Year-to-Date Trading and Outlook
The year-on-year revenue trend in Q3 improved showing a c.3% decline vs c.7% decline in H1. Lower shipment volumes as the Rugs business transitions manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey accounted for over half of the revenue decline in Q3. This was partially offset by ongoing market share gains and customer wins in UK Carpets, and a strong performance in Australia in particular. Excluding Rugs, year-on-year revenue declined approximately 1.5% in Q3.
Trading in the first half of January, however, was significantly impacted by weak consumer confidence and weak footfall at our end customers due to geopolitical events across our key markets: western Europe; North America and the UK. Whilst recent weeks have shown improvements in trading, the board now expects Q4 revenue to be below its previous expectations and approximately 5% below FY25.
As a result, the board expects post-IFRS16 EBITDA to be approximately £95m for FY26 as a whole*.
Progress on EBITDA improvement initiatives
Management's immediate focus remains on delivering EBITDA improvement initiatives within our control.
The first sales from the new V4 ceramics line in Spain are being delivered in Q4, which will drive growth and improved EBITDA in our Spanish ceramics business through FY27 and beyond.
The relocation of Rugs manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey also continues to progress in line with expectations, albeit shipping disruptions have been greater than anticipated.
The first stages of integrating our UK Underlay businesses and Australian businesses announced at the HY results are also expected to be completed before the end of March.
Whilst a lower starting point on volume will reduce the outlook for 2027, the currently disclosed EBITDA improvement initiatives remain on track, and further EBITDA improvements have been identified across the divisions and will be quantified as part of the ongoing budget process.
The board and business recognises the need to continue to adapt to the lower volume environment and drive efficiency improvements ahead of both domestic and international competition within its local markets.
Increased rigor in tracking these improvements is being implemented along with broader governance improvements, and further detail of these initiatives will be provided in due course.
Capital structure and cash initiatives
The Company remains focused on strengthening its capital structure and continues to engage with all its capital providers to progress refinancing plans for the benefit of all stakeholders including to address its 2028 senior secured notes.
The cash initiatives outlined at the half year results are advancing. Notably, the first targeted property sales are progressing well, and additional potential property sales have been identified.
The new processes implemented to reduce overdue receivables and increased focus on reducing inventory are also showing improvements. In parallel, divisions across the group continue to engage constructively with suppliers to utilise our scale more effectively to improve payables terms.
*
The Company considers that market expectations prior to the release of this announcement for FY26 to have been revenue of £1064m and
post-IFRS16 EBITDA of £110.7m
The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcementon behalf of the Company is Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer.
For more information contact:
Victoria PLC
Geoff Wilding, Executive Chairman
Philippe Hamers, Group Chief Executive
Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer
www.victoriaplc.com/investors-welcome
Via Edelman Smithfield PR

+44 (0)20 3207 7800
Edelman Smithfield (Investor Relations)
Alex Simmons
+44 (0)7970 174 252 or
alex.simmons@edelmansmithfield.com
About Victoria PLC
(
www.victoriaplc.com
)
Established in 1895 and listed since 1963 and on AIM since 2013 (VCP.L), Victoria PLC, is an international manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring products. The Company, which is headquartered in Worcester, UK, designs, manufactures and distributes a range of carpet, rugs, flooring underlay, ceramic tiles, LVT (luxury vinyl tile), artificial grass and flooring accessories.
Victoria has operations in the UK, Spain, Italy, Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, Turkey, the USA, and Australia and employs over 5,000 people across more than 30 sites. Victoria is Europe's largest carpet manufacturer and the second largest in Australia, as well as the largest manufacturer of underlay in both regions.
The Company's strategy is designed to create value for its shareholders and is focused on consistently increasing earnings and cash flow per share via acquisitions and sustainable organic growth.

company_30s.what_they_do: VCP.L is a specialist ceramics manufacturer that designs, produces and supplies decorative tiles, sanitary ware and related building materials to the UK and European construction markets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a sector that grows with every new housing build and refurbishment programme. Its products are used in both residential and commercial projects, giving it exposure to the broader construction cycle and the rising demand for energy‑efficient, low‑water fixtures.
company_30s.current_state: VCP has been in a tough spot – heavy debt, a shrinking order book and a recent CEO change. The latest RNS shows a new 130m super‑senior facility and a fresh ceramics line, signalling a pivot toward higher‑margin products. The stock trades at a fraction of its historic highs, reflecting the market’s scepticism about whether the turnaround can be sustained.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent filings paint a picture of a firm in transition: a new production line that should lift capacity and quality, a refinancing package that extends debt maturities, and a board that has just swapped the CEO. These moves indicate management is actively trying to stabilise cash flow and reduce leverage.

Two themes emerge. First, the focus on new, potentially higher‑margin production suggests a strategy shift. Second, the refinancing and extension of secured notes show an effort to shore up liquidity and avoid immediate distress.

If the new line can hit its projected output and the refinancing holds, the company could start to generate positive cash flow and stop the current cash burn. That would be the first tangible catalyst for a bounce.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical chatter with a flicker of cautious hope
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts highlight the new production line and debt extension as signs that the company may finally escape its low‑price trap.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many voices warn that the debt load remains high and that the company’s track record of missed targets could keep the price depressed.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from flat to mildly positive – a few new bullish comments appear after the latest RNS, but overall sentiment is still muted.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest is currently driving the price, which is a perfect setup for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A successful launch of the new ceramics line in Q3, with production reaching at least 70% of the targeted throughput.
A positive earnings announcement in the next quarterly report that shows a turnaround in cash flow or a significant reduction in debt covenants.
Any announcement of a strategic partnership or large construction contract that could lift demand for the company’s high‑margin products.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm the thesis include: a sustained increase in traded volume (≥30% above the 20‑day average), a clear break above the 50‑day moving average, and a positive earnings surprise of at least 20% versus analyst expectations.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late phase of capitulation – it has already dropped well below 70% of its peak and volume has tapered off. A mean‑reversion bounce would likely need a clear catalyst (such as a successful product launch) before the price can start climbing out of the compression zone.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is a classic deep‑crash bottom (≈80% down). The new production line and debt refinancing create a tangible turnaround catalyst. If the price recovers to the mid‑range of the 52‑week low‑high, the upside could be roughly 200% (from 2p to 6p). The risk is limited to the current trough – a 70% drop from the peak – so the upside‑to‑downside ratio is about 2.5:1.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still carries significant debt and has a history of missing earnings targets. If the new line fails to meet output or the refinancing falls through, the stock could continue sliding toward a 0p scenario or face a forced sale of assets. The worst case is a total wipe‑out of equity value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200% (2p → 6p). Potential downside: 70% (peak → trough). The asymmetry is favourable: a 2.5:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 50‑day moving average, ideally after a volume spike of at least 30% over the 20‑day average. Use a position size that limits the trade to 2–3% of your total portfolio. Place a stop just below the 200‑day moving average to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a confirmed earnings beat or a production‑line milestone announcement. If the price closes above the 50‑day MA with volume >30% and the 200‑day MA remains intact, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a default on its secured notes, a bankruptcy filing, or a forced asset sale, the trade would be terminated immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VCP.L sits on an 80% crash that, coupled with a new production line and debt extension, offers a 200% upside with a 70% downside, making it a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian play.
